result.markdown:
---
title: "data.frame stuff in dplyr vs. pandas"
description: "r & python"
draft: true
---


### load data

::: panel-tabset

### dplyr


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
# subset a few columns for simplicity
sw <- starwars[, 1:7]
```
:::


### pandas

::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
# this imports the sw dataset from the R code chunk
sw = r.sw
```
:::

:::

### Subset rows and columns by index

::: panel-tabset

### dplyr

`slice` rows by index, `select` columns by index or name.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sw %>% 
      # rows
      slice(1:3) %>% 
      # columns
      select(1:3)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
# A tibble: 3 x 3
  name           height  mass
  <chr>           <int> <dbl>
1 Luke Skywalker    172    77
2 C-3PO             167    75
3 R2-D2              96    32
```
:::
:::


### pandas

`.iloc`, think *integer location* to subset rows and columns. For subsetting
by name, use `loc`.


::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
(sw   # rows
      .iloc[0:3]
       # columns
      .iloc[:, 0:3])
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
             name  height  mass
0  Luke Skywalker     172  77.0
1           C-3PO     167  75.0
2           R2-D2      96  32.0
```
:::
:::


:::

### Subset rows by condition

::: panel-tabset

### dplyr

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sw %>% 
      filter((mass > 100) & (height > 200))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
# A tibble: 4 x 7
  name        height  mass hair_color skin_color   eye_color     birth_year
  <chr>        <int> <dbl> <chr>      <chr>        <chr>              <dbl>
1 Darth Vader    202   136 none       white        yellow              41.9
2 Chewbacca      228   112 brown      unknown      blue               200  
3 Grievous       216   159 none       brown, white green, yellow       NA  
4 Tarfful        234   136 brown      brown        blue                NA  
```
:::
:::


### pandas

::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
sw[(sw.mass > 100) & (sw.height > 200)]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
           name  height   mass  ...    skin_color      eye_color birth_year
3   Darth Vader     202  136.0  ...         white         yellow       41.9
12    Chewbacca     228  112.0  ...       unknown           blue      200.0
76     Grievous     216  159.0  ...  brown, white  green, yellow        NaN
77      Tarfful     234  136.0  ...         brown           blue        NaN

[4 rows x 7 columns]
```
:::
:::


:::

### Group by and summarise

::: panel-tabset

### dplyr

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sw %>% 
      group_by(eye_color) %>% 
      summarise(mass_median = median(mass, na.rm=TRUE),
                heigh_median = median(height, na.rm=TRUE))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
# A tibble: 15 x 3
   eye_color     mass_median heigh_median
   <chr>               <dbl>        <dbl>
 1 black                80            188
 2 blue                 81.5          180
 3 blue-gray            77            182
 4 brown                79            180
 5 dark                 NA             NA
 6 gold                 NA            191
 7 green, yellow       159            216
 8 hazel                66            174
 9 orange               74            184
10 pink                 NA            180
11 red                  90            190
12 red, blue            NA             96
13 unknown              31.5          136
14 white                48            178
15 yellow               80            175
```
:::
:::


### pandas

::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
(sw
      .groupby('eye_color')
      .agg({'mass': 'median', 'height': 'median'}))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
                mass        height
eye_color                         
black           80.0  1.830000e+02
blue            81.5  1.800000e+02
blue-gray       77.0  1.820000e+02
brown           79.0  1.770000e+02
dark             NaN -2.147484e+09
gold             NaN  1.910000e+02
green, yellow  159.0  2.160000e+02
hazel           66.0  1.700000e+02
orange          74.0  1.840000e+02
pink             NaN  1.800000e+02
red             90.0  1.900000e+02
red, blue        NaN  9.600000e+01
unknown         31.5  7.900000e+01
white           48.0  1.780000e+02
yellow          80.0  1.750000e+02
```
:::
:::


:::
